  "findings": [
    "Staged conductors are undersized for the specified load and run length, which will cause excessive voltage drop and potential overheating."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Reject the staged conductors and select a larger gauge wire that accounts for both the continuous load ampacity and the voltage drop over the run length."
  ],
  "rationale": "The staged conductors do not meet the minimum sizing requirements when factoring in both the load current and the voltage drop over the distance of the run. Although Table 310.16 provides allowable ampacities, voltage drop must be calculated to ensure the voltage at the point of use remains within acceptable limits (typically 3% for branch circuits and 5% overall).",
  "references": [
    "NEC 310",
    "NEC Table 310.16",
    "NEC 210.19(A) Informational Note No. 4"
  ]
}
